reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2: Attack path is via P2P RPC (data_columns_by_root), which is UNTRUSTED per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. Gate 3: Code reading verified - put_rpc_custody_columns (L240-267 data_availability_checker.rs) performs KZG verification but does NOT call observe_gossip_data_column, while gossip path (L521 data_column_verification.rs) does update observed_column_sidecars cache. Gate 4: An attacker can trigger this via P2P by publishing column C1 via gossip (gets cached in observed_column_sidecars at slot S, proposer P, index I), then serving conflicting column C2 (same S/P/I, different data, valid signature) via RPC to specific peers. RPC path only updates observed_slashable, not observed_column_sidecars, allowing both columns to coexist. Gate 5: 01e invariant PROP-6a4369e9-inv-013 (outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, lines 298-315) explicitly requires 'Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing' with assertion 'forall col: imported(col) => anti_equivocation_cache.contains(col.key)'. Code violates this by skipping cache update on RPC import path. Severity: HIGH maintained - allows equivocation attacks undermining PeerDAS data availability guarantees, though limited to single proposer's columns per slot.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Anti-equivocation cache must be updated for all imported data columns regardless of publishing. (Assertion: forall col: imported(col) => anti_equivocation_cache.contains(col.key))
